term:army: Finally, we can really live by the saying "An army marches on its stomach"
Like in how army composition affected the tactics a flank used in CK2.
term:siege: Will there be any restrictions on your character's actions if his main castle is under siege
And does it mean that, if you siege down fortified Baronies, you are immediately given control of other Baronies in the County, without the need to enter them?
term:system: I do kind of like the tactics system in CK2
Seems like the systems of CK2 are improved
term:knights: As for Knights, we'll have to see how exactly will they impact battles and, perhaps more importantly, how battles impact them
Siege engines, supply, besieger is attacker, knights
term:armies: So armies don't have 3 commanders 1 for each flank anymore?
I hope we won't have anymore crazy armies running around my realm trying to go for my capital when they should start by assaulting, you know, my borders.
term:war: Really liking the amount of effort in improving warfare
The context was always very clear and more importantly, in contrast to EU4, you didn't stand to lose half your realm if you lost an offensive war.
edge:attrition+supply: If my army has low supply and taking attrition, will the losses I take be proportionally dispersed among all "classes" of troops
And also no counter siege weapon like bannerlord where the defender can build up their defense to break the siege by increasing attrition over time to simulate medieval era supply thing
edge:cavalry+infantry: Tactically speaking, the pike square hadn't been invented yet, and cavalry often used lances longer than the pikes of the infantry
I've read that cavalry were the effective counter to pikemen, not the other way around (and pikes were very strong against other infantry).
edge:flank+tactics: Like in how army composition affected the tactics a flank used in CK2.
Will there be flanks, tactics and combat phases (melee and skirmish)
edge:commander+trait: Can a commander have multiple commander traits and will his normal/commander traits also affect troop bonuses depending on his martial like CK2?
Or maybe some commanders, for example, with a character trait: a coward, weak, would have a tendency to betray the Lord.
edge:knight+title: I don't want to micro manage 30-40 minor titles and then these knights only have the power of 300-400 levies
In turn you can probably reduce the amount of possible knights available as minor titles
edge:character+title: ------------------ 'Knight' is now a minor title you can bestow on characters in your realm with high enough combat prowess
very nice dd, I just hope that characters that only have the title of knight are labeled as Sir, I just think it would make the character look cooler
phrase:siege weapons: And siege weapons make an appeareance too, I like it
I didn't even expect to get something as cool as siege weapons
phrase:middle ages: Any non-trivial force in the Middle Ages would have had upwards of a hundred mounted warriors
Just saying spears are what you'd find heavy infantry equipping themselves with in the Middle Ages, they wouldn't really be using swords except as a personal defence or backup weapon.
phrase:siege engines: Siege engines, supply, besieger is attacker, knights
Are siege engines like ships, in the way that your soldiers transform into them, or do you have to move them along the map
phrase:late middle ages: You may also note that peasant levies were a common military power base for many Swedish leaders against the Danish king in the wars regarding the Kalmar Union in the late Middle Ages
every definition I found apply to serf, slaves as well as other farmers (sometimes even including freeman and landholders) to add onto that not every army functioned like it was the late middle ages
phrase:men-at-arms and knights: I like the men-at-arms and knights, but the loss of flanks (and, from the looks of it, the skirmish phase) takes some of the character out of CK battles.
I have a feeling that aside your own men attached to your demesne, at the the core men-at-arms and knights will be what your vassals bring with them to the army
